# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image 
FROM node:alpine   # Alpine Linux provides the smallest possible size of your Docker images, which is beneficial for performance and cost-effectiveness in production environments (~10 MB) with minimal dependencies installed on top level nodes that are used to build other containers. It also ensures a consistent environment across different machines by using an official Node.js image from Alpine Linux's package repository
 
# Set the working directory inside container as /app   # This is where your app will be mounted in host machine and docker run command, it can access our code base at this location within Dockerfile context of running process (./) by default. It also allows us to use relative paths from current file's position
WORKDIR /app 
# Copy package dependencies into the container image   # This is where we install all other packages that are required for Node app, using apt-get in Dockerfile which will be done inside RUN command below. The list of these requirements can also reside at top level and used to build docker images with different versions or tags
COPY package*.json ./  # Copying the dependencies from current directory (./) into container image as they are required for Node app, using apt-get in Dockerfile which will be done inside RUN command below. The list of these requirements can also reside at top level and used to build docker images with different versions or tags
 
# Install the dependencies from package*.json file   # This is where we install all other packages that are required for Node app, using apt-get in Dockerfile which will be done inside RUN command below. The list of these requirements can also reside at top level and used to build docker images with different versions or tags
RUN npm install  # Run the following commands within container image as they would run if we were running this script directly from host machine (./Dockerfile) using Docker CLI command below. The list of these requirements can also reside at top level and used to build docker images with different versions or tags
